meta.title: German words

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Every word and meaning transcribed faithfully; context sentences and the teaching layer are authored for CAT OS.

meta.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Word DNA milestone); WORD_DNA_BIBLE frameworks applied
meta.reviewer_notes: The batch's first foreign-word unit; carries the WORD_DNA_BIBLE §3a honesty note that borrowed words are met, not decoded. Complete German list from the source, seven of seven words. Owner review pending.

unit.label: German
unit.mentor_note: These aren't patterns to crack. They're words English simply borrowed from German, whole. Meeting them is the whole skill.

members.context_sentence: She packed thick slices of pumpernickel for the picnic.

members.meaning: a school or class for young children
members.context_sentence: The children in the kindergarten class are learning their letters.
A group of hoodlums had been breaking streetlights all week.

members.meaning: a shop selling prepared and imported foods
members.context_sentence: We bought smoked fish and olives from the delicatessen on the corner.
Dew made the grass glisten in the early morning light.
He kicked off his loafers and walked barefoot on the sand.

members.meaning: a minor and usually temporary malfunction
members.context_sentence: There was a strange glitch in the software, so the screen froze for a second.

discovery.notice_prompt: English borrowed every one of these words from German. Read each one in its sentence, then guess before the reveal.

predict_options.text: A school or class for young children
A large garden used for growing vegetables
A German dessert made with fruit

discovery.understand_note: Kinder means children and garten means garden in German. English borrowed the whole phrase and narrowed it to one specific use: a class for young children, the same warm image, a smaller frame.

applies.prompt: "He kicked off his loafers and walked barefoot on the sand." What are loafers?

options.text: A casual style of slip-on shoe
A type of lazy, unemployed person

applies.prompt: "There was a strange glitch in the software, so the screen froze for a second." What does glitch mean here?

options.text: A small, temporary malfunction or fault
A deliberate feature added by the designer
